features.name: Everything CLI hook rewrites file-search commands to es

the command is rewritten to use "es" with equivalent arguments

scenarios.name: Hook intercepts two sequential file-search commands from the same agent session

both commands are rewritten to use "es"

the hook-execution log entry for this tool call shows "rg-hook: no-match"

scenarios.name: WRONG hook order — rg-hook before es-hook causes silent wrong-tool rewrite with no error

steps.text: the rg-hook is registered BEFORE the es-hook in the ".claude/settings.json" PreToolUse hooks array (wrong order)
an agent issues a Bash tool call containing "find . -name '*.ts' | grep 'TODO'"
the rg-hook fires first (it appears at an earlier array index) and matches on "grep"
the rg-hook rewrites the command to start with "rg" (file-search intent served by text-search tool)
the es-hook receives the rg-rewritten command as its input and produces no-match (no file-search trigger present)
the final executed command starts with "rg" — not "es"
no error or warning is surfaced to the agent
no [WARN] line is written to the pipeline log (stdout)
the hook-execution log entry shows "rg-hook: match | result: rewrite"
the hook-execution log entry shows "es-hook: no-match"
the es-hook is registered as a PreToolUse hook in ".claude/settings.json"
the rg-hook is registered as a PreToolUse hook in ".claude/settings.json"

features.name: Hook rewrite can complete after pipelineState transitions to "halted" — D27 observable

scenarios.name: Pipeline halts due to routing failure while hook rewrite is in-flight — hook rewrite completes

steps.text: a pipeline is running with hookState="intercepting" (the es-hook is mid-rewrite for a "find . -name '*.ts'" command)
concurrently the routing subsystem encounters a missing "config/model-routing.psd1" file
the routing failure sets pipelineState to "halted"
the es-hook rewrite completes before the pipeline controller terminates the hook process
hookState transitions to "done" (the rewrite completed successfully despite the pipeline halt)
pipelineState remains "halted" (the routing failure terminal state — not recoverable)
hookState="done" and pipelineState="halted" are jointly absorbing
the pipeline log (stdout) contains the routing-failure halt error entry for the missing config file
the hook-execution log records the completed rewrite entry (hookState="done" reached before process termination)
the pipeline does NOT transition to "done" — "halted" is the terminal state regardless of hook completion

steps.text: an agent session was invoked via Invoke-Claude with Role "code-writer"
the Claude CLI process launched by Invoke-Claude exits (agent session ends)
the close hook runs "tsx graph/index.ts" before the next pipeline stage begins

scenarios.name: Close-hook sequencing contract — next pipeline stage must not begin until close hook completes

steps.text: an agent session ends (the Claude CLI process launched by Invoke-Claude exits)
the close hook "tsx graph/index.ts" begins executing
no subsequent pipeline stage begins executing while the close hook is running
the subsequent pipeline stage begins only after the close hook process exits with any exit code
the ordering contract holds regardless of whether the close hook succeeds or fails

scenarios.name: Close-hook sequencing VIOLATION — detectable observable when next stage begins prematurely

steps.text: an agent session has ended and the close hook "tsx graph/index.ts" is running
the close hook has NOT yet exited (the tsx process is still executing)
the pipeline controller prematurely starts the next pipeline stage before the close hook exits
the pipeline log (stdout) contains a next-stage-start log entry
the timestamp of the next-stage-start log entry is BEFORE the timestamp of the close hook process exit
this timestamp ordering constitutes a detectable sequencing violation
a sequencing violation detector (integration test or pipeline harness check) can observe this as a test failure

scenarios.name: Close hook fires exactly once per agent — first session (elicitor)

steps.text: an elicitor agent session completes (the Claude CLI process launched by Invoke-Claude for the elicitor exits)
the elicitor agent session ends
the close hook fires exactly once for the elicitor session
the close hook does not fire zero times (missing exactly-once guarantee)
the close hook does not fire twice (double-fire guarantee)
the "tsx graph/index.ts" process is invoked exactly once for this elicitor session

scenarios.name: Close hook fires exactly once per agent — second sequential session (code-writer)

steps.text: an elicitor agent session has already completed in this pipeline run (close hook fired once for it)
a code-writer agent session now completes (the Claude CLI process launched for the code-writer exits)
the code-writer agent session ends
the close hook fires exactly once for the code-writer session
the close hook does not fire zero times for the code-writer session
the close hook does not fire twice for the code-writer session
the "tsx graph/index.ts" process has been invoked exactly 2 times total across the pipeline run (once per completed agent session)

scenarios.name: Close hook generates CLAUDE.md with node sections

steps.text: the graph contains file nodes "packages/vibe-cli/vibe.ps1", "packages/vibe-cli/utils/invoke-claude.ps1", "packages/vibe-cli/config/state-repository.psd1"
the close hook runs "tsx graph/index.ts"
the root "CLAUDE.md" contains a "## Files" header
the "## Files" section lists "packages/vibe-cli/vibe.ps1, packages/vibe-cli/utils/invoke-claude.ps1, packages/vibe-cli/config/state-repository.psd1" as comma-separated items

scenarios.name: Close hook generates CLAUDE.md with edge sections

steps.text: the graph contains a "calls" edge from "packages/vibe-cli/vibe.ps1" to "packages/vibe-cli/utils/invoke-claude.ps1"
the graph contains a "depends_on" edge from "packages/vibe-cli/utils/invoke-claude.ps1" to "packages/vibe-cli/config/state-repository.psd1"
the close hook runs "tsx graph/index.ts"
the root "CLAUDE.md" contains a "## calls" header
the "## calls" section lists "packages/vibe-cli/vibe.ps1 -> packages/vibe-cli/utils/invoke-claude.ps1"
the root "CLAUDE.md" contains a "## depends_on" header
the "## depends_on" section lists "packages/vibe-cli/utils/invoke-claude.ps1 -> packages/vibe-cli/config/state-repository.psd1"

scenarios.name: Close hook overwrites existing CLAUDE.md

steps.text: a root "CLAUDE.md" exists with prior graph content
the close hook runs "tsx graph/index.ts"
the root "CLAUDE.md" is overwritten with the current graph state
prior content is fully replaced

scenarios.name: Next agent reads updated CLAUDE.md from previous agent's close hook

steps.text: agent A's close hook wrote a "CLAUDE.md" containing nodes "packages/vibe-cli/vibe.ps1" and "packages/vibe-cli/utils/invoke-claude.ps1"
agent B starts
agent B's context includes the "CLAUDE.md" with nodes "packages/vibe-cli/vibe.ps1" and "packages/vibe-cli/utils/invoke-claude.ps1"

scenarios.name: Empty graph produces CLAUDE.md with section headers and no items

steps.text: the knowledge graph has no nodes or edges
the close hook runs "tsx graph/index.ts"
the root "CLAUDE.md" is written
the file contains section headers but no items

scenarios.name: Atomic write mechanism — close hook writes graph output to a temp file then renames to CLAUDE.md

steps.text: the close hook is configured to write "CLAUDE.md" atomically
the close hook runs "tsx graph/index.ts"
the graph output is written to a temp file in the repo root directory first
the temp file is renamed to "CLAUDE.md" as the final atomic step

scenarios.name: Crash during temp-file write preserves prior CLAUDE.md intact — no partial file at canonical path

steps.text: a prior "CLAUDE.md" exists at the repo root containing valid graph output
the tsx process crashes during the temp-file write phase before the atomic rename completes
the canonical "CLAUDE.md" at the repo root remains intact and unchanged from the prior successful build
no partial or truncated content appears at the canonical "CLAUDE.md" path
the pipeline log records a [WARN] entry indicating the close-hook failed before completing the atomic rename

scenarios.name: Atomic write temp-file rename failure — prior CLAUDE.md remains intact, temp file persists on disk

steps.text: a prior "CLAUDE.md" exists at the repo root containing valid graph output
the close hook writes the temp file to the repo root directory successfully
the atomic rename of the temp file to "CLAUDE.md" fails (e.g., "CLAUDE.md" is locked by another process)
the canonical "CLAUDE.md" at the repo root remains intact and unchanged from the prior successful build
the temp file remains on disk (it was written successfully before the rename failed)
a [WARN] line is written to the pipeline log (stdout) describing the rename failure
the pipeline continues
markdownState remains "stale" (the canonical CLAUDE.md is the prior version, not the new content)

scenarios.name: Final-agent close hook success transitions graphState to "done" — pipeline termination condition met

steps.text: a pipeline run with MaxAgents=2 where the first agent's close hook has already succeeded (agentsCompleted=1)
the second (final) agent session completes its work and its close hook runs "tsx graph/index.ts" successfully
the final agent's close hook exits with exit code 0 (GraphWriteSuccess fires; agentsCompleted increments from 1 to 2)
agentsCompleted reaches MaxAgents (agentsCompleted=2 = MaxAgents=2)
graphState transitions to "done" (NOT back to "collecting" — the terminal branch fires because agentsCompleted >= MaxAgents)
the pipeline termination condition is met: routingState=done ∧ graphState=done ∧ hookState∈{idle,done,error}
the pipeline reaches its terminal "done" state (pipelineState=done)
no [WARN] line is written to the pipeline log (stdout) for the final agent's close hook

scenarios.name: tsx process hangs during close hook — pipeline continues after closeHookTimeout expires

steps.text: an agent session has ended (Invoke-Claude returns)
the close hook starts "tsx graph/index.ts"
the tsx process does not exit within the configured closeHookTimeout
the close hook terminates the hung tsx process after the timeout expires
a [WARN] line is written to the pipeline log (stdout) indicating the close-hook timed out
the pipeline continues to the next stage
the stale "CLAUDE.md" from the previous successful build persists unchanged
markdownState remains "stale" — closeHookTimeout leaves the canonical CLAUDE.md from the prior successful build unmodified

the second agent's close hook also fails with a non-duplicate compilation error (markdownState remains "none")
a third agent starts, discovers node "packages/vibe-cli/vibe.ps1", and its close hook succeeds
the third agent's close hook runs "tsx graph/index.ts" successfully

the "CLAUDE.md" contains node "packages/vibe-cli/vibe.ps1"
no [WARN] line is written to the pipeline log (stdout) for the third agent's close hook
subsequent agents read the up-to-date "CLAUDE.md" reflecting the current graph state

a subsequent agent starts, discovers node "packages/vibe-cli/config/model-routing.psd1", and its close hook succeeds

the root "CLAUDE.md" is overwritten with the current graph state (markdownState transitions from "stale" to "current")
the "CLAUDE.md" contains node "packages/vibe-cli/config/model-routing.psd1"

subsequent agents read the up-to-date "CLAUDE.md" reflecting the current graph state

scenarios.name: Mid-pipeline GraphWriteFail returns graphState to "collecting" — not "warn" (B9)

steps.text: a pipeline run with MaxAgents=3 and agentsCompleted=1 (the first agent's close hook has already completed)
the second agent's session ends and its close hook runs "tsx graph/index.ts"
the second agent's close hook fails with a non-duplicate compilation error
GraphWriteFail fires for the second agent (agentsCompleted+1 = 2 < MaxAgents = 3)
graphState transitions to "collecting" — NOT to "warn" (the ELSE branch fires because agentsCompleted+1 < MaxAgents)
agentsCompleted is incremented to 2 (GraphWriteFail still increments agentsCompleted regardless of the graphState branch)
retryCount is reset to 0 (TLA+ GraphWriteFail sets retryCount'=0 in BOTH the terminal IF branch and this ELSE branch — epoch-boundary reset fires regardless of which graphState branch executes)
the pipeline continues — graphState="collecting" means the third agent can still run its close hook
a [WARN] line is written to the pipeline log (stdout) for the second agent's close-hook failure
the stale "CLAUDE.md" from the prior successful build persists unchanged

scenarios.name: Mid-pipeline GraphWriteSuccess returns graphState to "collecting" — not "done" (B-3)

steps.text: a pipeline run with MaxAgents=3 and agentsCompleted=1 (the first agent's close hook has already completed)
the second agent's session ends and its close hook runs "tsx graph/index.ts" successfully
GraphWriteSuccess fires for the second agent (agentsCompleted+1 = 2 < MaxAgents = 3)
graphState transitions to "collecting" — NOT to "done" (the ELSE branch fires because agentsCompleted+1 < MaxAgents)
agentsCompleted is incremented to 2 (GraphWriteSuccess increments agentsCompleted in both IF and ELSE branches)
retryCount is reset to 0 (TLA+ GraphWriteSuccess sets retryCount'=0 at epoch-end in both branches)
graphOpsCount is reset to 0 for the next agent epoch (TLA+ GraphWriteSuccess sets graphOpsCount'=0 in both branches)
the canonical "CLAUDE.md" is overwritten with the second agent's graph output (GraphWriteSuccess completed the write)
markdownState transitions to "current" (the successful write updates the markdown state)
the pipeline continues — graphState="collecting" means the third agent can still run its close hook
no [WARN] line is written to the pipeline log (stdout) for the second agent's close hook (the write succeeded)

the vibe-cli pipeline process crashes (e.g., PowerShell process exits unexpectedly) after epoch 1 close hook completed but before epoch 2 begins

the "CLAUDE.md" at the repo root is still present and unchanged from epoch 1's successful write
the file contains node "packages/vibe-cli/vibe.ps1" — the epoch-1 graph content is intact
the restarted pipeline agent reads the preserved "CLAUDE.md" and receives epoch-1 graph context

scenarios.name: Pipeline abort while graphState="collecting" — GraphHaltCleanup preserves prior CLAUDE.md (markdownState UNCHANGED)

steps.text: a prior "CLAUDE.md" exists at the repo root containing valid graph output (markdownState="stale" or "current")
the graph subsystem is in graphState="collecting" (the agent session is ongoing; no close-hook write is in progress)
the pipeline controller receives an abort signal and pipelineState transitions to "halted"
graphState transitions to "warn" via the GraphHaltCleanup halt-cleanup path

a [WARN] line is written to the pipeline log (stdout) indicating the pipeline was aborted while the graph was collecting

scenarios.name: Pipeline abort while graphState="writing" with markdownState="stale" entry — GraphHaltCleanup leaves markdownState "stale" (OBJ-11a)

steps.text: a prior "CLAUDE.md" exists at the repo root from an earlier successful build (markdownState="stale" — the most recent close hook attempt failed)
the close hook has begun writing the temp file (graphState="writing")
the pipeline controller receives an abort signal and pipelineState transitions to "halted"
the canonical "CLAUDE.md" at the repo root remains intact and unchanged
markdownState is "stale" — GraphHaltCleanup leaves markdownState UNCHANGED (TLA+ S33 markdownState ∈ UNCHANGED); the stale entry value is preserved
graphState transitions to "warn" via the GraphHaltCleanup halt-cleanup path (not GraphWriteFail)
a [WARN] line is written to the pipeline log (stdout) indicating the close-hook was interrupted by pipeline abort
the temp file (if partially written) does not appear at the canonical "CLAUDE.md" path

scenarios.name: Pipeline abort while graphState="writing" with markdownState="current" entry — GraphHaltCleanup leaves markdownState "current" (OBJ-11b)

steps.text: the most recent close hook succeeded (markdownState="current" — the canonical "CLAUDE.md" reflects the current graph)
a subsequent agent's close hook has begun writing the temp file (graphState="writing")
the pipeline controller receives an abort signal and pipelineState transitions to "halted"
the canonical "CLAUDE.md" at the repo root remains intact and unchanged
markdownState is "current" — GraphHaltCleanup leaves markdownState UNCHANGED (TLA+ S33 markdownState ∈ UNCHANGED); the current entry value is preserved
graphState transitions to "warn" via the GraphHaltCleanup halt-cleanup path (not GraphWriteFail)
agentsCompleted is NOT incremented — GraphHaltCleanup bypasses the close-hook write and leaves the counter unchanged
a [WARN] line is written to the pipeline log (stdout) indicating the close-hook was interrupted by pipeline abort
the temp file (if partially written) does not appear at the canonical "CLAUDE.md" path

scenarios.name: Aggregate invariant — markdownState="current" implies agentsCompleted>=1 and most recent close hook succeeded

steps.text: a pipeline run where the most recent agent's close hook ran "tsx graph/index.ts" successfully
the close hook exited with exit code 0 (GraphWriteSuccess fired)
agentsCompleted was incremented to at least 1 by this or a prior successful close hook
markdownState is observed as "current" (the canonical "CLAUDE.md" reflects the current graph state)
agentsCompleted is at least 1 — markdownState="current" cannot be reached with agentsCompleted=0
the most recent close hook completed without a non-duplicate compilation error — graphState is NOT "warn" for the most recent write
the "CLAUDE.md" at the repo root was written by the GraphWriteSuccess path (not by any GraphWriteFail or GraphHaltCleanup path)
an agent reading "CLAUDE.md" in this state receives a up-to-date graph context from the most recent successful epoch

scenarios.name: Pipeline abort with markdownState="current" — GraphHaltCleanup leaves markdownState unchanged (stays "current")

steps.text: the most recent agent's close hook succeeded (markdownState="current" — the canonical "CLAUDE.md" is up-to-date)
the graph subsystem is in graphState="collecting" (no close-hook write is in progress)
the pipeline controller receives an abort signal and pipelineState transitions to "halted" (GraphHaltCleanup fires)
markdownState REMAINS "current" — the abort did not trigger a write attempt; the canonical "CLAUDE.md" is not touched
graphState transitions to "warn" via GraphHaltCleanup
agentsCompleted is NOT incremented — GraphHaltCleanup bypasses the close-hook write; the counter is left unchanged
the canonical "CLAUDE.md" at the repo root remains the same file written by the most recent successful close hook
a subsequent agent reading "CLAUDE.md" in this aborted pipeline state would still see up-to-date graph content
a [WARN] line is written to the pipeline log (stdout) indicating the pipeline was aborted

scenarios.name: Graph build failure during pipeline propagates warning and stale CLAUDE.md persists

the current agent's close hook fails with a non-duplicate compilation error (graphState=warn)
the next pipeline stage begins
the next agent reads the stale "CLAUDE.md" from the previous successful build
the pipeline does not halt

scenarios.name: E2E dedup-retry — agent discovers duplicate node, retries, succeeds, pipeline completes

steps.text: a fresh pipeline run with an empty knowledge graph
the model mapping maps "code-writer" to "sonnet"
the es-hook and rg-hook are registered in ".claude/settings.json"
the code-writer agent is invoked via Invoke-Claude with Role "code-writer"
the agent issues "find . -name '*.ts'" (rewritten to "es" by the es-hook)
the agent calls .addNode() with type "file" and id "packages/vibe-cli/vibe.ps1" introducing a duplicate
the build fails and the error with retryCount=0 and path "packages/vibe-cli/vibe.ps1" is sent back to the agent
the agent removes the duplicate .addNode() call for "packages/vibe-cli/vibe.ps1"
the graph is rebuilt via "tsx graph/index.ts" and succeeds
the Claude CLI process exits (agent session ends)
the close hook runs "tsx graph/index.ts" and writes the updated "CLAUDE.md"
the root "CLAUDE.md" contains node "packages/vibe-cli/vibe.ps1"
the pipeline continues to the next stage
no force-dedup entry is present in the pipeline log (stdout)

scenarios.name: E2E triple failure — routing fails after prior hook error and graph warning, pipeline halts

steps.text: a pipeline run seeded with hookState=error from a prior agent invocation
a pipeline run seeded with graphState=warn from a prior agent invocation
the seeded state is verified: hookState=error and graphState=warn are both confirmed before the When step executes
the file "config/model-routing.psd1" does not exist for the current invocation
Invoke-Claude is called with Role "code-writer" for the next pipeline stage
the pipeline halts with a missing-config error
routingState is "error" (the routing subsystem failed — mapping file not found)
pipelineState is "halted" — not "done" (routing failure is the only path to pipelineState="halted")
the pipeline log (stdout) contains a routing-failure error entry for the missing config file
a [WARN] entry for the close-hook failure from the prior invocation is present in the pipeline log (stdout)
graphState is "warn" from the prior close-hook failure (the graph subsystem reached its warn terminal state)
